title: Where Enterprises are Actually Adopting AI
body: Analysis of enterprise AI adoption patterns across Fortune 500 and Global 2000 companies
Focus on leading AI startups and sector-specific penetration rates

title: Overview: Key Themes
body: Enterprise penetration rates in Fortune 500 and Global 2000
Coding sector dominance in AI adoption
Legal AI startup success stories
Revenue concentration across use cases
Acceleration timeline post-ChatGPT launch

title: Fortune 500 AI Adoption Rate
body: 29% of the Fortune 500 are live, paying customers of a leading AI startup
This represents significant penetration among the largest U.S. companies
These are active deployments, not pilot programs or trials

title: Global 2000 AI Adoption Rate
body: Approximately 19% of the Global 2000 are live, paying customers of a leading AI startup
Lower penetration rate compared to Fortune 500 reflects varied global readiness
Still represents hundreds of major enterprises actively using AI solutions

title: Coding Sector Dominance
body: The coding sector dominates AI enterprise adoption by nearly an order of magnitude
This dominance surpasses all other enterprise AI use cases combined
Represents the clearest product-market fit in enterprise AI to date

title: Leading Coding AI Tools
body: Companies like Cursor and Claude Code are growing faster than most predictions
These tools represent the fastest-growing segment of enterprise AI adoption
Growth rates exceed analyst expectations and market forecasts

title: Harvey: Legal AI Success Story
body: Harvey, a legal AI startup, achieved approximately $200 million in annualized recurring revenue
This milestone was reached within three years of operation
Represents one of the fastest revenue ramps in legal technology history

title: Eve: Plaintiff Law AI Platform
body: Eve, specializing in plaintiff law, surpassed 450 customers
The company reached a $1 billion valuation
Demonstrates strong demand for specialized legal AI beyond corporate law

title: Legal Sector AI Adoption
body: Both Harvey and Eve demonstrate rapid enterprise adoption in legal services
Legal AI represents a significant category beyond the coding sector
Specialized legal applications show different adoption patterns than general-purpose tools

title: Revenue Distribution: Top Three Categories
body: Coding, support, and search represent the lion's share of enterprise AI adoption by revenue
These three use cases dominate commercial success in enterprise AI
Other categories remain significantly smaller in revenue terms

title: Coding's Revenue Dominance
body: Coding represents the largest revenue category in enterprise AI adoption
The sector's dominance is nearly an order of magnitude over other categories
Developer tools show the strongest willingness-to-pay among enterprise buyers

title: Support and Search Revenue
body: Support and search, along with coding, represent the lion's share of enterprise AI revenue
These categories demonstrate clear ROI for enterprise buyers
Customer support and enterprise search show strong adoption alongside coding

title: ChatGPT's Launch as Catalyst
body: The speed of enterprise AI adoption accelerated dramatically following ChatGPT's November 2022 launch
November 2022 represents an inflection point for enterprise awareness and deployment
Post-launch acceleration exceeded pre-ChatGPT adoption trajectories

title: Enterprise AI Adoption Outlook
body: Nearly one-third of Fortune 500 companies now actively use leading AI tools
Coding sector shows order-of-magnitude leadership with continued rapid growth
Legal AI demonstrates billion-dollar outcomes in specialized verticals
Three core use cases drive majority of enterprise AI revenue today
